title: ERROR: Assert `j < i' failed in kernel/mem.cc:495
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

I'm attempting to port a MiSTer core (Intellivision) to an ECP5 board.  The project's predominantly VHDL so I'm using Yosys and the GHDL plugin (including some very recent ghdl bugfixes).

I've run the code through bugpoint to create an MCVE in RTLIL, attached here:

[result.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/8848648/result.zip)

The top module is called intv_core, so to reproduce the issue, use the following command:
```
yosys -p "read_rtlil result.il; synth_ecp5 -top intv_core"
```

## Expected behavior
Either completion of the synthesis process, or an error message detailing the signal / module being processed if something's wrong with the design file, or if something about it is unsupported.

## Actual behavior
The synthesis stops with the following message:
```
3.24. Executing MEMORY pass.

3.24.1. Executing OPT_MEM pass (optimize memories).
ERROR: Assert `j < i' failed in kernel/mem.cc:495.
```
code_blocks:
3.24. Executing MEMORY pass.

3.24.1. Executing OPT_MEM pass (optimize memories).
ERROR: Assert `j < i' failed in kernel/mem.cc:495.
